data.title: Apple Store app adds 'virtual shopping assistant' to improve experience
data.description:
The Apple (AAPL) Store app appears to be in the process of adding an artificial intelligence agent to help users with their shopping experience, according to a legal update posted on their website.

The virtual shopping assistant features a chat interface that helps users purchase devices based on details like account information, device identities, carrier information, location, prior purchases and chat information.

"Where the Apple Store app virtual shopping assistant is available, Apple collects and stores your account information, device identifiers, carrier information, chat information, and, where enabled, location data to personalize the chat experience, provide relevant responses, and, if you opt in, to improve the virtual shopping assistant," Apple said. "Before sharing the chat with our partners, Apple scrubs personal identifiers from it. These partners will use this information only to help Apple to provide a conversational response. Apple will save these transcripts to make them available when you return to the Apple Store app, for our business analytics, and if you opt in, to improve the virtual shopping assistant."

Apple is also in the process of adding a virtual assistant for its Apple Support app.

data.title: RBC Capital downgrades Northern Trust to Hold-equivalent after Q2 earnings
data.description:
Northern Trust (NTRS) was cut to Hold-equivalent at RBC Capital Markets on valuation and expected slower revenue growth in 2027.

Shares were 2.37% lower at $174.42 before the market open on Thursday.

The premier trust company's second-quarter revenue surged on fee growth, capital markets, and net interest income.

"Though NTRS posted very strong Q2 results, led by strong revenue growth combined with positive operating leverage, the stock exceeds our price target, and with expected slower revenue growth in 2027, we have lowered our rating to Sector Perform," said the investment bank in a research note. The previous rating was Outperform.

"NTRS maintained its medium-term strategic goals, including a mid-teens ROE, a 33% pre-tax margin, and expenses-to-trust fees of 105-110%, exceeding two out of three goals in Q2," said analysts Gerard Cassidy and Thomas Leddy.

"When the current 'bull market' ends, NTRS should be able to navigate around the challenges better than most of its peers," added the research note.

RBC Capital's rating aligns with Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating and the Wall Street analysts rating of Hold. SA authors grade the stock as Buy.

data.title: NVR GAAP EPS of $83.96 misses by $6.02, revenue of $2.33B misses by $30M
data.description:
• NVR press release (NVR): Q2 GAAP EPS of $83.96 misses by $6.02.
  • Revenue of $2.33B (-10.4% Y/Y) misses by $30M.
  • Homebuilding revenues of $2.28 billion in the second quarter of 2026 decreased by 11% compared to homebuilding revenues of $2.55 billion in the second quarter of 2025.
  • New orders in the second quarter of 2026 increased by 9% to 5,885 units, when compared to 5,379 units in the second quarter of 2025.
  • The average sales price of new orders in the second quarter of 2026 was $437,100, a decrease of 5% when compared to the second quarter of 2025. 
  • The cancellation rate in the second quarter of 2026 was 15% compared to 17% in the second quarter of 2025. 
  • Settlements in the second quarter of 2026 decreased by 8% to 5,058 units, compared to 5,475 units in the second quarter of 2025.
  • The average settlement price in the second quarter of 2026 was $450,700, a decrease of 3% when compared to the second quarter of 2025.
  • Backlog of homes sold but not settled as of June 30, 2026 increased on a unit basis by 9% to 10,998 units and increased on a dollar basis by 5% to $4.99 billion when compared to the respective backlog unit and dollar balances as of June 30, 2025.
  • Shares -2% PM.

data.title: ACG leases six Boeing 737-10 jets to Aerolíneas Argentinas
data.description:
Aviation Capital Group said Thursday it has signed lease agreements with Aerolíneas Argentinas for six Boeing (BA) 737-10 aircraft, with the first delivery scheduled for 2028.

The aircraft will support the Argentine flag carrier's fleet renewal and expansion plans, adding capacity from its Buenos Aires hubs at Aeroparque Jorge Newbery Airport and Ezeiza Airport on domestic and regional routes across South America.

The announcement was at the Farnborough International Airshow, an event that is traditionally one of the aviation industry's biggest venues for airlines, aircraft lessors and manufacturers to unveil aircraft orders and leasing agreements. Investors closely watch the show for signs of airline demand, production trends and the competitive positions of Boeing and Airbus.

The six aircraft will come from ACG's existing Boeing 737-10 orderbook. The lessor said the first aircraft is expected to be delivered in 2028.

Aerolíneas Argentinas said the aircraft form part of its 2027-2031 fleet plan. The airline said the larger-capacity narrowbody jets will help increase seats on high-demand domestic and regional routes while improving operating efficiency.

The Boeing 737-10 is the largest member of the 737 MAX family. The model has not yet entered commercial service and remains subject to certification by the U.S. Federal Aviation Administration.

ACG said it was a launch customer for the 737-10 and has delivery positions extending from an anticipated first delivery in late 2026 through 2033. The company said it is among the largest lessors of the aircraft variant.

data.title: J&J multiple myeloma drugs Tecvayli and Talvey boost cancer-free survival in late-stage trial
data.description:
Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) announced on Thursday that Tecvayli and Talvey, its FDA-approved treatments for the blood cancer multiple myeloma, provided greater benefit in reducing the risk of cancer progression or death as part of a combination regimen in a late-stage trial.

Based on topline data from its Phase 3 MonumenTAL-6 study, the New Jersey-based pharma giant said Tecvayli in combination with Talvey led to an 89% reduction in the risk of disease progression or death compared to a 73% reduction for Tecvayli + the anticancer agent pomalidomide.

The three-arm study was designed to evaluate Tecvayli + Talvey and Tecvayli + pomalidomide versus the investigator's choice of standard of care in adults with relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma who have undergone up to four prior lines of therapy.

The MONUMENTAL-6 study reached the primary endpoint, indicating statistically significant and clinically meaningful improvements in progression-free survival for both investigational arms and overall survival, a key secondary endpoint, according to JNJ.

The overall safety profiles for investigational arms were in line with the established safety profiles of each drug, J&J (JNJ) said, adding that given the strong results, MonumenTAL-6's independent Data Monitoring Committee recommended unblinding the study at the initial interim analysis.

data.title: IBM's outlook sees positive reactions from analysts after Q2 results
data.description:
Analysts maintained their positive views on IBM (IBM) after providing better-than-expected guidance following preliminary second quarter results last week that resulted in a stock drop of about 25%.

Shares of IBM dipped about 2% premarket on Thursday.

J.P. Morgan kept its Overweight rating and $250 price target on the shares.

"Results were relatively in-line with our revised forecast post-preannouncement, with better than expected guidance for the full year, but IBM now needs to execute to build investor confidence in the outlook. IBM’s preannouncement last week sparked greater levels of concern about the durability of the company’s mainframe and related software business as enterprises shift investment toward AI infrastructure," said analysts led by Brian Essex.

The analysts noted that commentary on the call implied confidence that 'tens of large deals,' which drove the miss for the quarter, were slipped deals rather than lost deals, with a third of those deals already closing this month. The analysts added that IBM adjusted its full-year outlook to reflect 4% to 5% constant currency growth for the year (from over 5% year-over-year previously) as budget pressure related to server, storage, and memory inflation that the analysts have consistently been hearing about year-to-date increased materially in the last few weeks of the second quarter.

"We remain Overweight rated as we think the current stock price reflects a more dire outlook many likely assumed after the preannouncement, but expect investors will need to see evidence of better execution before underwriting a meaningful recovery," said Essex and his team.

RBC Capital maintained its Outperform rating and $270 price target on the shares. 

"IBM delivered results and guidance which aligned with preliminary numbers last week as well as the narrative around a short-term capex disruption. We expected to see more conservatism applied to revenue guidance than FCF, but both numbers were above our expectations raising the bar for the 2H/26," said analyst Matthew Swanson and his team.

Jefferies kept its Buy rating and $260 price target on the stock.

"The quarter was disappointing, but the rapid recovery of roughly one-third of slipped mainframe deals and reiterated FCF guide support mgmt's view that timing, rather than demand, drove much of the shortfall. IBM’s recurring software base and intact FCF framework provide support, while delayed mainframe and ELA transactions offer a potential 3Q recovery catalyst, though we would avoid giving full credit for the maintained guide until a larger portion of the slipped activity is reflected in reported results," said analysts led by Brent Thill.
